title: toronia toru
intro: Toronia toru, also known as the New Zealand tōtara tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its unique, large leaves and distinctive trunk. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Toronia toru include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
